company.name: Camurus
company.subtitle: Buvidal/Brixadi opioid dependence | Oczyesa acromegaly EU launched | Oclaiz PDUFA Jun 10, 2026 | Lilly incretin deal

indications.name: Opioid use disorder (EU Buvidal + US Brixadi royalties)

market.notes: Buvidal own sales in EU/Australia + Brixadi US royalties from Braeburn. Brixadi ~30% US LAI buprenorphine market share. Target: 100K Buvidal patients by 2027. UK funding issues temporary headwind. 2026 guide SEK 2.6-2.9B total revenue.

us.reachPct.note: Brixadi (buprenorphine ER SC) launched 2023 via Braeburn; 8% of ~2M US OUD on MAT vs Sublocade dominant (~60%) and generic Suboxone (~30%).

us.priceK.note: $25K/yr WAC; parity with Sublocade ($1,750/mo); Medicaid PDL placement key.

us.wtpPct.note: 60% -- payors cover as MAT; REMS program; weekly/monthly flexibility vs Sublocade monthly only.

eu.reachPct.note: Buvidal launched 2018 EU via Camurus; 18% of ~1.5M EU OUD in MAT; Nordics/UK early leaders.

eu.wtpPct.note: 70% -- OUD treatment prioritized by health services; UK NHS widely adopted.

row.reachPct.note: 5% -- AU launched; partial LATAM; Asia limited.

us.patientsK.note: SAMHSA/NIDA 2024: ~2M US OUD; ~500K on MAT (buprenorphine, methadone, naltrexone).

row.patientsK.note: WHO: ~40M global OUD; treatment access highly variable.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% - Medicare/Medicaid cover buprenorphine (Suboxone), Sublocade, Vivitrol; X-waiver eliminated 2023 expanding access.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~60% - EU methadone/buprenorphine OAT programs; Sublocade LAI expanding.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% - LMICs minimal MAT access; stigma and regulation barriers.

us.priceK.note: Blended $5K: buprenorphine generic (Suboxone) $2K (70%), Sublocade LAI $22K, Vivitrol $15K, methadone clinic $5K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$2.5K blended; EU methadone cheap, buprenorphine generic.

penPct.note: ~25% peak branded - generic buprenorphine dominates; LAI (Sublocade, Brixadi) growth in treatment retention.

assets.name: Oczyesa/Oclaiz (CAM2029) — octreotide SC depot (acromegaly)
assets.stage: Commercial EU (launched Nov 2025) + US PDUFA Jun 10, 2026

market.notes: Monthly SC octreotide depot. EU approved, launched Germany Nov 2025. US NDA resubmitted, PDUFA Jun 10, 2026. Superior to first-gen SRL (Sandostatin LAR). 3-5K EU acromegaly patients targetable. FluidCrystal technology.

us.patientsK.note: Ipsen/Crinetics 10-Ks + Acromegaly Community: ~25K US acromegaly prevalence; ~8K on somatostatin analogs (Sandostatin, Somatuline, Mycapssa).

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet acromegaly: ~30K EU; ESE registry; ~10K treated with SSA/pegvisomant.

row.patientsK.note: Global acromegaly ~90K prevalence; treatable ex-US/EU ~15K (Japan/AU registries).

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% -- Medicare/commercial cover Sandostatin LAR, Somatuline Depot, Mycapssa oral, pegvisomant (Somavert); Crinetics paltusotine Ph3.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- NICE/G-BA reimburse SSAs + pegvisomant second-line; oral Mycapssa growing.

us.priceK.note: $80K/yr blended: Sandostatin LAR ($45K), Somatuline Depot ($55K), Mycapssa oral ($70K), Somavert ($120K); paltusotine (Crinetics) Ph3 pricing TBD.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$45K post AMNOG/NICE SSA pricing.

row.priceK.note: $20K blended -- Japan NHI branded, emerging octreotide generic.

penPct.note: ~20% peak -- oral paltusotine (Crinetics Ph3) + Mycapssa shift drives SSA switch pool expansion.

us.reachPct.note: OCZYESA (octreotide SC depot) US launched 2025 via Camurus partner; 15% of ~8K US acromegaly pts vs Sandostatin LAR/Somatuline entrenched.

us.priceK.note: $48K/yr WAC; parity with Signifor LAR/Somatuline (~$45K); self-administration reduces HCP cost.

us.wtpPct.note: 55% -- payors accept weekly SC convenience vs monthly IM; PA similar to incumbents + prior SSA failure not required.

eu.reachPct.note: Launched 2023 EU; 20% of ~10K EU acromegaly; Germany/UK/Italy early uptake.

eu.wtpPct.note: 65% -- G-BA recognized additional convenience benefit; AMNOG negotiated.

eu.priceK.note: $32K/yr net; Germany ~35K EUR, UK NICE approved.

market.notes: SORENTO Phase 3 vs Sandostatin LAR. Primary PFS readout mid/H2 2026. Event accrual slower than expected (patients doing well). Major TAM expansion from acromegaly.

us.patientsK.note: ~12K US GEP-NET prevalent on-tx; ~4K incident/yr. Source: NCI SEER 2024, NET Research Foundation.

us.wtpPct.note: ~62% -- Sandostatin/Somatuline SSA 1L; Lutathera (Lu-177-DOTATATE) PRRT NETTER-1/2; Afinitor/Sutent mid-grade; Medicare broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE + G-BA approved lanreotide + Lutathera; ESMO + ENETS guidelines.

row.wtpPct.note: ~16% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL octreotide biosim.

us.priceK.note: Blended $110K: SSA $45K (55% pts) + Lutathera $190K (25%) + Afinitor/Sutent $165K (15%) + chemo $30K (5%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$65K blended; EU net ~59% US; octreotide LAR generic lowers.

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- SSA + Lutathera saturated grade-dependent; next-gen PRRT (Ac-225) + combos (Lutathera+nivo) extend.

us.reachPct.note: CAM2029 (octreotide SC) NET P3 SORENTO hit 2024; 12% of ~15K US GEP-NET pts; Somatuline/Sandostatin LAR dominant.

us.wtpPct.note: 55% -- payors follow NCCN SSA guidelines; Ki-67 <20% criteria; SC convenience vs IM depot valued.

market.notes: POSITANO Phase 2b positive: significant liver cyst volume reduction. Orphan designation in ADPKD (US+EU). No approved therapy for PLD. 37K patients US/EU4/UK. Phase 2/3 planned.

us.patientsK.note: Otsuka Jynarque 10-K + NIH: ~140K US ADPKD; ~20K PLD (polycystic liver disease) with significant hepatomegaly treatment-eligible.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet ADPKD: ~160K EU; PLD treatable ~25K per ERA-EDTA registry.

row.patientsK.note: Global ADPKD ~12M prevalence; treatable pool ROW ~30K PLD.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Medicare/commercial cover Jynarque (tolvaptan) for ADPKD progression; PLD novel agents (somatostatin analogs) step-edited.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE/G-BA reimburse tolvaptan for rapid progression ADPKD Mayo Class 1C-1E.

row.wtpPct.note: ~25% -- Japan NHI covers tolvaptan; emerging markets octreotide/lanreotide off-label.

us.priceK.note: $13K/yr anchored to Otsuka Jynarque WAC + octreotide/lanreotide (~$20K) for PLD off-label.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$7K post HTA negotiation; Jynarque ~$8K, somatostatin ~$12K.

row.priceK.note: $4K blended -- Japan tolvaptan reimbursed, emerging generic/biosimilar.

penPct.note: ~15% peak -- PLD-specific pipeline limited; tolvaptan mature for ADPKD.

us.reachPct.note: CAM2043 (treprostinil SC) PLD ADPKD P2 2025; 5% of ~40K US symptomatic PLD if approved 2028; somatostatin analogs off-label SOC.

us.wtpPct.note: 40% -- payors require imaging-confirmed PLD + symptoms; orphan-adjacent.

market.notes: Lilly deal: up to $870M milestones + royalties for FluidCrystal long-acting incretins (up to 4 compounds, including dual GIP/GLP-1 and triple agonists). CAM2056 monthly semaglutide Phase 1 completed enrollment. If monthly/quarterly GLP-1 works: massive opportunity.

us.patientsK.note: CDC NHANES 2023: 42% US adults BMI>=30 = ~110M; ~40M eligible for anti-obesity medications (AOMs) post-insurance criteria.

eu.patientsK.note: WHO Europe 2023: ~150M obese in EU+UK; ~30M eligible for AOMs given stricter HTA BMI/comorbidity gates.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~450M obese; ~50M eligible (China, Japan, AU have growing GLP-1 reimbursement).

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Medicare excludes weight-loss indication (Part D statute); commercial coverage expanding post-SELECT CV outcomes.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- NICE/G-BA limited to BMI>=35 + comorbidities; most purchase out-of-pocket.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan PMDA approved, Medicare-equivalent coverage; emerging markets mostly self-pay.

us.priceK.note: $15K/yr avg across Mounjaro ($13K), Zepbound ($12K), Wegovy ($16K), Saxenda ($15K); oral GLP-1 pending.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$8K; post-G-BA negotiation Zepbound/Wegovy in UK, Germany ~50% of US list.

row.priceK.note: $3K blended; Japan PMDA pricing ~$9K, emerging markets ~$1-2K via rebates/tiered pricing.

penPct.note: ~5% peak reflects capacity-constrained launch curve; supply expansion through 2028 unlocks more.

us.reachPct.note: Camurus-Lilly FluidCrystal incretin depot collab announced 2024; 2% of ~110M US obese if Lilly advances tirzepatide/retatrutide depot formulation post-2028.

us.priceK.note: $14K/yr WAC modeled; parity with weekly GLP-1 list.

us.wtpPct.note: 35% -- payors limit GLP-1 access by BMI/comorbidity; depot convenience does not expand coverage.

mega_bear.narrative: Buvidal stalls + Oclaiz US rejected + SORENTO fails + Lilly deprioritizes
events: X UK funding issues persist — Buvidal growth stalls
X FDA rejects Oclaiz again — manufacturing issue
X SORENTO GEP-NET misses PFS endpoint
X Lilly incretin formulations don't meet standards
-> Revenue declines, stock to SEK 300

bear.narrative: Buvidal grows modestly + Oclaiz approved but slow US launch + SORENTO borderline
events: V Buvidal grows but UK/Germany headwinds persist
V Oclaiz approved US — but uptake slow vs entrenched Sandostatin
~ SORENTO shows PFS trend but not statistically significant
~ Lilly partnership advancing but timelines long

base.narrative: Oclaiz US approved + Buvidal/Brixadi growing + SORENTO positive + PLD advancing
events: V Oclaiz approved Jun 2026 — US acromegaly launch
V Buvidal/Brixadi combined 80K+ patients by 2027
V SORENTO positive — GEP-NET label adds major TAM
V PLD Phase 2/3 initiates — first approved therapy potential
~ Lilly incretin partnership progressing

bull.narrative: FluidCrystal platform delivers across OUD + rare endocrine + Lilly incretins — SEK 5B+ revenue
events: V Buvidal 100K+ patients — global OUD standard
V CAM2029 across acromegaly + GEP-NET + PLD = SEK 2B+ franchise
V Lilly incretin milestones flowing — monthly GLP-1 validated
V Revenue SEK 5B+ by 2029, 40%+ operating margins

psychedelic_bull.narrative: FluidCrystal becomes the dominant long-acting drug delivery platform — Novo/Lilly acquires
events: V CAM2029 franchise SEK 4B+ across 3+ indications
V Lilly monthly incretin becomes standard — $500M+ royalties to Camurus
V FluidCrystal platform licensed for 10+ molecules globally
V Novo Nordisk or Lilly acquires for SEK 60-80B

lastData.title: SEK 2.3B 2025 revenue (+21%), Oczyesa EU launched, Oclaiz PDUFA Jun 10
points: Buvidal product sales + Brixadi royalties growing
Oclaiz US NDA resubmitted, PDUFA Jun 10, 2026
POSITANO Phase 2b PLD positive — Phase 2/3 planned
Lilly FluidCrystal deal: up to $870M milestones + royalties

nextCatalyst.title: Oclaiz US PDUFA + SORENTO GEP-NET primary readout H2 2026
thresholds: Oclaiz PDUFA Jun 10, 2026 — US acromegaly approval
SORENTO Phase 3 GEP-NET primary PFS endpoint mid/H2 2026

risks: SEK 28B market cap at ~12x revenue — premium for specialty pharma
UK government funding issues impacting Buvidal adoption
Oclaiz US has been rejected before — resubmission risk
SORENTO event accrual slower than expected
Lilly incretin partnership is early — years from revenue contribution
Brixadi US royalties depend on Braeburn execution
Competition in acromegaly from Ipsen Somatuline + generic octreotide
Swedish-listed with limited US investor awareness
PLD is unproven market — regulatory path uncertain

catalysts.title: Oclaiz/CAM2029 PDUFA decision
catalysts._rationale: FDA assigned PDUFA target action date of June 10, 2026 for NDA resubmission

catalysts.title: SORENTO GEP-NET Phase 3 readout
catalysts._rationale: Updated timeline shows SORENTO primary readout targeted for H2 2026, event-driven.
